explanation_en: 'Erfahrung' means 'experience'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: Experience is the best teacher.

explanation_en: 'Erfahrung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ zeigt, dass Geduld wichtig ist.
question.en: Experience shows that patience is important.

explanation_en: 'Erfahrung' means 'experience'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Er hat viele Jahre _____ in diesem Beruf.
question.en: He has many years of experience in this profession.

explanation_en: 'Erfahrung' is used without an article in this context — expressing an uncountable amount of experience.
'Entwicklung' means 'development'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The development of technology is impressive.

explanation_en: 'Entwicklung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The development of the economy is going well.

explanation_en: 'Entwicklung' means 'development'. It is the feminine subject, used with 'die'.

question.en: We have been observing this development for years.

explanation_en: 'beobachten' takes the accusative. Feminine nouns look the same in nominative and accusative: 'diese Entwicklung'.
'Bedeutung' means both 'meaning' (of a word) and 'importance'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.de: _____ Bedeutung dieses Wortes ist mir unklar.
question.en: The meaning of this word is unclear to me.

explanation_en: 'Bedeutung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The importance of this decision is enormous.

explanation_en: 'Bedeutung' means 'importance' here. It is the feminine subject, used with 'die'.

question.de: Ich verstehe die _____ dieses Satzes nicht.
question.en: I don't understand the meaning of this sentence.

explanation_en: 'verstehen' takes the accusative. Feminine: 'die Bedeutung' (same in accusative as nominative).
'Möglichkeit' means 'possibility'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The possibility still exists.

explanation_en: 'Möglichkeit' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____, ins Ausland zu reisen, ist teuer.
question.en: The possibility of travelling abroad is expensive.

explanation_en: 'Möglichkeit' means 'possibility'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Wir haben keine _____ mehr, das zu ändern.
question.en: We no longer have the possibility of changing that.

explanation_en: 'keine Möglichkeit' — 'keine' negates the feminine noun in the accusative. The noun form is unchanged.
'Veränderung' means 'change'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The change was unexpected.

explanation_en: 'Veränderung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ des Klimas macht uns Sorgen.
question.en: The change in the climate worries us.

explanation_en: 'Veränderung' means 'change'. It is the feminine subject, used with 'die'.

question.en: I welcome this change very much.

explanation_en: 'begrüßen' takes the accusative. Feminine nouns look the same in nominative and accusative.
'Entscheidung' means 'decision'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The decision was correct.

explanation_en: 'Entscheidung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The decision is now up to you.

explanation_en: 'Entscheidung' means 'decision'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: He deeply regrets his decision now.

explanation_en: 'bereuen' takes the accusative. 'seine Entscheidung' — feminine accusative is the same form as nominative.
'Gesellschaft' means 'society'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: Society is changing.

explanation_en: 'Gesellschaft' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: Society must act together.

explanation_en: 'Gesellschaft' means 'society'. It is the feminine subject.

question.de: Er hat viel für die _____ getan.
question.en: He has done a lot for society.

explanation_en: 'für' takes the accusative. Feminine: 'die Gesellschaft' (same in accusative).
'Wirtschaft' means 'economy'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The economy is growing slowly.

explanation_en: 'Wirtschaft' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ des Landes ist sehr stark.
question.en: The economy of the country is very strong.

explanation_en: 'Wirtschaft' means 'economy'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: The government wants to boost the economy.

explanation_en: 'ankurbeln' takes the accusative. Feminine: 'die Wirtschaft' (same form in accusative).
'Wissenschaft' means 'science'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.de: _____ Wissenschaft hilft uns, die Welt zu verstehen.
question.en: Science helps us to understand the world.

explanation_en: 'Wissenschaft' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: Science is always making new discoveries.

explanation_en: 'Wissenschaft' means 'science'. It is the feminine subject, used with 'die'.

question.de: Er hat sein Leben der _____ gewidmet.
question.en: He devoted his life to science.

explanation_en: 'widmen' with dative: 'der Wissenschaft' — feminine dative uses 'der'. The noun is unchanged.
'Sprache' means 'language'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: Language is an important means of communication.

explanation_en: 'Sprache' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die deutsche _____ hat viele interessante Regeln.
question.en: The German language has many interesting rules.

explanation_en: 'Sprache' means 'language'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: I am currently learning a new language.

explanation_en: 'lernen' takes the accusative. 'eine neue Sprache' — feminine indefinite accusative: 'eine'.
'Kultur' means 'culture'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The culture of a country is unique.

explanation_en: 'Kultur' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ Deutschlands ist reich und vielfältig.
question.en: The culture of Germany is rich and diverse.

explanation_en: 'Kultur' means 'culture'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Ich interessiere mich für die _____ anderer Länder.
question.en: I am interested in the culture of other countries.

explanation_en: 'interessieren für' takes accusative. Feminine: 'die Kultur' (unchanged in accusative).
'Umgebung' means 'surroundings' or 'environment' (immediate area around something). Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.de: _____ Umgebung der Stadt ist sehr grün.
question.en: The surroundings of the city are very green.

explanation_en: 'Umgebung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The surroundings here are very quiet.

explanation_en: 'Umgebung' means 'surroundings'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: We are exploring the surroundings of the city.

explanation_en: 'erkunden' takes the accusative. Feminine: 'die Umgebung' (unchanged in accusative).
'Lösung' means 'solution'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The solution has finally been found.

explanation_en: 'Lösung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The solution is simpler than expected.

explanation_en: 'Lösung' means 'solution'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Wir suchen eine kreative _____ für dieses Problem.
question.en: We are looking for a creative solution to this problem.

explanation_en: 'suchen' takes the accusative. 'eine kreative Lösung' — feminine indefinite accusative: 'eine'.
'Aufgabe' means 'task' or 'exercise'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The task is not easy.

explanation_en: 'Aufgabe' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ muss bis Freitag fertig sein.
question.en: The task must be finished by Friday.

explanation_en: 'Aufgabe' means 'task'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Der Chef gibt ihr eine wichtige _____ .
question.en: The boss gives her an important task.

explanation_en: 'geben' takes dative (ihr) + accusative (Aufgabe). 'eine wichtige Aufgabe' — feminine indefinite accusative.
'Wirkung' means 'effect'. It is a feminine noun taking 'die'.

question.en: The effect of the medicine is strong.

explanation_en: 'Wirkung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ des Trainings ist nach zwei Wochen sichtbar.
question.en: The effect of the training is visible after two weeks.

explanation_en: 'Wirkung' means 'effect'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Das Ereignis hatte eine große _____ auf die Öffentlichkeit.
question.en: The event had a great effect on the public.

explanation_en: 'hatte eine Wirkung' — 'haben' takes accusative. 'eine große Wirkung' — feminine indefinite accusative.
